diseaseName: Rotator Cuff Tendinopathy / Shoulder Pain Syndrome

prevalence.trend: increasing with aging and occupational load

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical exam; imaging (US/MRI) for suspected tear or refractory cases

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: tens of millions with shoulder pain episodes
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; access to physiotherapy is limited in many areas

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis common; imaging access concentrated in urban centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Exercise/physiotherapy, NSAIDs, injections; surgery in selected cases in tertiary centers
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited physio access and delayed imaging/specialist referral
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Low-cost exercise-based rehab and growing orthopedic services
